notes: This pass continued from the repo-forward seam at C3:2800 using the same caller-plus-local-structure discipline as the recent conservative C3 pages.

The page's strongest visible support is the double low-bank same-bank JSR pressure into 2809 from C3:0CC9 and C3:0CF4, which is materially better than the false one-off hits driving several immediately previous freezes.
Even so, 2809 still fails local byte-level sanity checks strongly enough to reject promotion; it opens into a branch-heavy control blob and does not settle into a defendable owner/helper body.

The other visible targets are worse: 2835 is only hit from obvious ASCII/text material at C3:0EE1, 282D is only hit from a mixed/data-side JMP at C3:D4DF, and the visible callers into 2802, 2848, 288E, and 28B0 are mixed-content or too structurally weak to rescue those starts.

The page also contains lone return-looking bytes at 285A, 286D, and 287C, but none has a caller-backed true start and all remain unsupported interior splinters rather than defensible stubs or wrappers.

Because the best caller-backed target is still structurally bad and the later return-looking bytes are unsupported interior fragments, the whole 2800..28FF page is frozen honestly as mixed inline content before the next 2900 seam.
